results.comment_text:
@user1:
I strongly suggest using `message.sender_chat` also in these cases. Not including a field called `sender_chat` in this property doesn't make much sense to me :D
results.test_code:
import pytest

from telegram import Chat, Message, Update


def test_effective_sender_includes_sender_chat_for_channel_post():
    """
    Regression test for Update.effective_sender:
    For channel posts, effective_sender should consider message.sender_chat (the channel chat),
    not fall back to effective_user (which is None for channel posts).
    """
    channel_chat = Chat(id=-100123456789, type=Chat.CHANNEL, title="Test Channel")

    channel_post = Message(
        message_id=1,
        date=0,
        chat=channel_chat,
        sender_chat=channel_chat,
        text="hello from channel",
    )

    update = Update(update_id=42, channel_post=channel_post)

    assert (
        update.effective_sender == channel_chat
    ), "Update.effective_sender must return channel_post.sender_chat for channel_post updates"

results.comment_text:
@user1:
I would have expected something like `assert isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` here making sure that we're getting the right attribute for the right use cases 🤔 

@author:
Right. this is why i intended to have two tests, although currently not working as you suggest..

`test_effective_sender_non_anonymous`: makes sure sender is either `User` or `None`
`test_effective_sender_anonymous`: makes sure sender is either `Chat`, `User` or `None`

i'll make them them more clear 😅
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pytest
from telegram import Chat, Update, User
import tests.test_update as ttu

def test_effective_sender_tests_assert_type_and_are_split_for_anonymous_cases__a1():
    """
    Regression test for review request:
    Ensure that tests for Update.effective_sender explicitly assert the type
    (Chat vs User) for the relevant use cases, and that there are separate tests
    for anonymous vs non-anonymous cases.

    This should fail for the "before" code because it only asserts `sender.id == 1`
    and has no `isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` assertions.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/tests/test_update.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'def test_effective_sender_non_anonymous' in source, 'Expected a dedicated test `test_effective_sender_non_anonymous` to exist, separating the non-anonymous case for `effective_sender`.'

def test_effective_sender_tests_assert_type_and_are_split_for_anonymous_cases__a2():
    """
    Regression test for review request:
    Ensure that tests for Update.effective_sender explicitly assert the type
    (Chat vs User) for the relevant use cases, and that there are separate tests
    for anonymous vs non-anonymous cases.

    This should fail for the "before" code because it only asserts `sender.id == 1`
    and has no `isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` assertions.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/tests/test_update.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'def test_effective_sender_anonymous' in source, 'Expected a dedicated test `test_effective_sender_anonymous` to exist, separating the anonymous case for `effective_sender`.'

def test_effective_sender_tests_assert_type_and_are_split_for_anonymous_cases__a3():
    """
    Regression test for review request:
    Ensure that tests for Update.effective_sender explicitly assert the type
    (Chat vs User) for the relevant use cases, and that there are separate tests
    for anonymous vs non-anonymous cases.

    This should fail for the "before" code because it only asserts `sender.id == 1`
    and has no `isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` assertions.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/tests/test_update.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'isinstance(sender, Chat)' in source or 'isinstance(sender,User)' in source or 'isinstance(sender, User)' in source, 'Expected tests for `effective_sender` to explicitly assert the sender type via `assert isinstance(sender, Chat)` and/or `assert isinstance(sender, User)`.'

def test_effective_sender_tests_assert_type_and_are_split_for_anonymous_cases__a4():
    """
    Regression test for review request:
    Ensure that tests for Update.effective_sender explicitly assert the type
    (Chat vs User) for the relevant use cases, and that there are separate tests
    for anonymous vs non-anonymous cases.

    This should fail for the "before" code because it only asserts `sender.id == 1`
    and has no `isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` assertions.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/tests/test_update.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    channel_update = Update(update_id=1, channel_post=ttu.channel_post)
    sender = channel_update.effective_sender
    assert isinstance(sender, Chat), 'Runtime sanity check: for channel_post updates, `effective_sender` should be a Chat.'

def test_effective_sender_tests_assert_type_and_are_split_for_anonymous_cases__a5():
    """
    Regression test for review request:
    Ensure that tests for Update.effective_sender explicitly assert the type
    (Chat vs User) for the relevant use cases, and that there are separate tests
    for anonymous vs non-anonymous cases.

    This should fail for the "before" code because it only asserts `sender.id == 1`
    and has no `isinstance(sender, Chat/User)` assertions.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/tests/test_update.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    channel_update = Update(update_id=1, channel_post=ttu.channel_post)
    sender = channel_update.effective_sender
    msg_update = Update(update_id=2, message=ttu.message)
    sender2 = msg_update.effective_sender
    assert isinstance(sender2, (Chat, User, type(None))), 'Runtime sanity check: `effective_sender` should be Chat/User/None depending on anonymity/use-case.'
